Name: Sun Devil Rocketry (SDR)
Relevance: ASU's primary high-power and liquid rocketry student organization; designs and launches research-grade rockets.

Name: Sun Devil Satellite Laboratory (SDSL)
Relevance: Student organization dedicated to the design, construction, and operation of small satellites and CubeSats.

Name: Students for the Exploration and Development of Space (SEDS ASU)
Relevance: Student chapter of the national SEDS organization promoting space exploration and development through projects and advocacy.

Name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (AIAA) ASU Branch
Relevance: Professional organization branch for students interested in aerospace engineering and atmospheric flight.

Name: Phoenix CubeSat Team
Relevance: Undergraduate-led mission that successfully launched a 3U CubeSat to study urban heat islands via thermal remote sensing.

Name: AstroDevs
Relevance: Student organization focusing on software development and computer science applications for astronomy and space missions.

Name: Interplanetary Initiative
Relevance: ASU-wide initiative to build the future of human space exploration through interdisciplinary research and student labs.

Name: ASU NewSpace
Relevance: Initiative that connects ASU research expertise with the commercial space industry and government agencies.

Name: Mars Space Flight Facility (MSFF)
Relevance: Research hub for Mars exploration, managing instruments like THEMIS and providing a public facility for Mars data analysis.

Name: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter Camera (LROC) SOC
Relevance: Science Operations Center for the LROC instrument on NASA's Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter, managing high-res lunar imaging.

Name: Buseck Center for Meteorite Studies (BCMS)
Relevance: Home to one of the world's largest university-based meteorite collections; conducts research on solar system origins.

Name: Interplanetary Initiative Lab (IPL)
Relevance: Technical lab supporting student missions like SquidSat and developing modular technologies for low-cost space access.

Name: SquidSat Team
Relevance: Student-led project under the Interplanetary Initiative developing a modular 3U CubeSat for payload testing.

Name: SPARCS (Star-Planet Activity Research CubeSat) Lab
Relevance: Research team building a dedicated ultraviolet space telescope in a CubeSat format to monitor exoplanet host stars.

Name: Ronald Greeley Center for Planetary Studies
Relevance: NASA Regional Planetary Information Facility providing data and support for planetary surface research.

Name: Center for Space Control
Relevance: Focuses on the control, guidance, and navigation of space systems, including satellites and robotic platforms.

Name: Low Frequency Cosmology Lab (LoCo Lab)
Relevance: Develops instrumentation and analyzes data to detect signals from the early universe (Cosmic Dawn), including space-based radio astronomy.

Name: ASU Drone Studio
Relevance: Advanced facility for testing autonomous UAVs and drone swarms with high-precision motion capture and avionics testing.

Name: Distributed Robotic Systems (DRS) Lab
Relevance: Researches coordination and control of robot swarms, applicable to planetary surface exploration and satellite constellations.

Name: Autonomous Systems and Robotics (ASR) Group
Relevance: Cross-disciplinary group focusing on path planning and autonomy for terrestrial and extraterrestrial robotic systems.

Name: ASU NASA Space Grant Consortium
Relevance: University-led program providing internships and funding for student research projects in aerospace and planetary science.

Name: Desert WAVE
Relevance: All-female robotics team designing autonomous underwater vehicles (AUV), utilizing sensors and GNC algorithms adjacent to space robotics.

Name: ASU Robotics Team
Relevance: Participates in NASA-sponsored competitions and other robotic challenges focused on autonomy and navigation.

Name: Center for Meteorite Studies (CMS)
Relevance: Focuses on the curation and analysis of meteorites to understand the formation and evolution of the solar system.

Name: ASU Cosmological Observatory
Relevance: Facility for astronomical observation and training students in telescopic techniques and astrophysics.

Name: Planetary Surface Composition Lab
Relevance: Analyzes the chemical and mineralogical composition of planetary surfaces using spectroscopy and remote sensing.

Name: Plasma Physics Lab
Relevance: Researches fundamental plasma physics, relevant to space weather, plasma propulsion, and astrophysical phenomena.

Name: Center for Embedded Systems
Relevance: Develops critical computing systems for aerospace, defense, and autonomous flight applications.

Name: Daedalus Astronautics (Historical)
Relevance: Founded in 2003, it was ASU's first major rocketry club and the direct predecessor to Sun Devil Rocketry.

Name: Space Photography Laboratory (Historical)
Relevance: Former lab focused on processing and archiving planetary mission photography, foundational to SESE's current remote sensing work.

Name: Psyche Mission Student Collaborations
Relevance: Undergraduate programs (Psyche Inspired, Psyche Capstone) allowing students to contribute to the NASA Psyche mission via art and engineering.

Name: Europa Clipper THEMIS Team
Relevance: ASU-led team managing the Thermal Emission Imaging System (THEMIS) for NASA's Europa Clipper mission.

Name: ASU Radio Astronomy Group
Relevance: Specializes in developing radio telescopes and observing low-frequency cosmic signals from space and Earth.

Name: Sun Devil Motorsports (FSAE)
Relevance: While automotive-focused, its work on high-performance telemetry, electronics, and composites is highly adjacent to aerospace engineering.

Name: Global Airborne Observatory (GAO)
Relevance: Utilizes advanced airborne sensors (imaging spectroscopy, LiDAR) for Earth observation, providing data adjacent to satellite remote sensing.
